meta.title: How to deploy your AudioMoth
meta.description: An interactive field guide to preparing, configuring, deploying and retrieving an AudioMoth recorder with Arbimon.

hero.title: How to deploy your AudioMoth
hero.lead: Prepare the recorder, configure a reliable schedule, connect the deployment to Arbimon and bring the recordings safely home.

video.title: Watch the field walkthrough
video.caption: See the full process before taking your equipment into the field.
video.frameTitle: Video: how to deploy an AudioMoth

prepare.heading: Before you start
prepare.intro: Use the checklists to make sure nothing important is left behind. Your progress is saved on this device, so it remains after you refresh the page.

equipment.cable: Micro USB data cable that fits your computer
equipment.case: Waterproof phone case or heavy-duty ziplock bag

manager.companion: Arbimon Companion App on the phone

field.companion: Arbimon Companion App on the phone

prepare.remaining: {count, plural, =0 {Everything is ready} one {# item left} other {# items left}}
prepare.linksNote: Open each app link while you still have a reliable internet connection.

account.heading: Create your Arbimon project
account.intro: Your project keeps recordings, locations and field notes together.
account.accountText: Go to Arbimon, choose Sign up or Start Analyzing, complete the form and verify your email.
account.projectText: Choose Create a new project, enter a unique project name and submit the form. The project will appear under My Projects.
account.tip: Choose a name your whole team will recognize, such as the landscape and survey period.

configure.heading: Configure the recorder
configure.intro: Connect the AudioMoth to your computer, then work through these four stations. Select a station to inspect it.
configure.flashText: Insert three AA batteries, connect the recorder with the Micro USB data cable and open the Flash App. Download the latest firmware, then choose Flash AudioMoth.
configure.timeTitle: Set the clock and label the device
configure.timeText: Open the Time App and choose Set Time. Find the Device ID, write down its first two and last two characters, then place that short ID on the recorder.
configure.recordText: In the Configuration App, enter the settings below. This records for one minute every five minutes and suits a two-week deployment.
configure.chimeValue: Always required when switching to CUSTOM
configure.recordWarning: For a different survey goal or timeframe, ask your bioacoustics lead before changing these settings.
configure.save: Choose Configure AudioMoth to save the settings.
configure.cardTitle: Label and insert the MicroSD card
configure.cardText: Label the card with a white pen or a thin sticker. Insert it into the AudioMoth until it clicks.
configure.formatNote: Cards up to 32 GB should use FAT32. Larger cards can use exFAT, but 32 GB or less is recommended.

test.heading: Test before leaving
test.intro: A two-minute test can prevent a wasted field trip.
test.s1: Move the switch to DEFAULT and let the recorder run for a few minutes.
test.s2: Move the switch back to USB/OFF.
test.s3: Remove the MicroSD card and insert it into the computer.
test.s4: Open the card and listen to one of the new audio files.
test.success: If the files are present and audible, the recorder is ready to deploy.

deploy.heading: Deploy with the Companion App
deploy.intro: Complete these steps at the deployment location. The app connects the right site, coordinates and photos to the recorder.
deploy.offline: Going offline? Sign in to the Companion App before leaving internet coverage. The deployment will sync when you reconnect.

s1.title: Open the deploy checklist
s1.text: Choose your project, tap the green plus button and select AudioMoth.

s2.title: Set the deployment site
s2.text: Choose Set deployment site, mark the recorder's location and confirm it.

s3.title: Sync the recorder
s3.text: Set the switch to USB/OFF and choose Begin Sync. When the tone plays, switch to CUSTOM, hold the phone close and minimize background noise. Confirm that the red light is not solid.

s4.title: Photograph and deploy
s4.text: Optionally add up to ten setup photos, then choose DEPLOY. A pin appears on the map after the deployment is saved.

tips.heading: Field placement tips
tips.water: Protect the recorder in a waterproof case made for water activities or in a heavy-duty sealed bag.
tips.camouflage: Use a camouflage case when available and place it away from casual view.
tips.rope: Secure the case to a tree with thin rope without blocking the microphone opening.
tips.tide: In mangroves, place it above the highest expected tide.
tips.sound: Keep the microphone clear of rubbing leaves, loose straps and other sources of handling noise.

retrieve.heading: Retrieve your AudioMoth
retrieve.intro: At the end of the survey, stop the recorder before opening its case.
retrieve.off: Move the switch from CUSTOM to USB/OFF.
retrieve.remove: Remove the MicroSD card and keep it with the recorder's short ID.
retrieve.check: Insert the card into a computer and listen to a few recordings before leaving the area, when possible.

upload.heading: Import the recordings
upload.intro: The GainForest team usually handles this step. Community partners can use this workflow when needed.
upload.s1: Insert the MicroSD card into the computer using a reader or adapter.
upload.s2: Open the Arbimon Uploader and sign in.
upload.s3: Choose Import Files and select the card or recording folder. You can also drag the folder into the uploader.
upload.s4: Confirm that the displayed project and site match the deployment.
upload.s5: Choose Import, then start the upload.
upload.s6: Keep the uploader open until every file has finished uploading.
upload.slow: Large surveys can take a long time to upload. It is fine to continue other work while the uploader runs.
upload.checkText: Open the Arbimon project dashboard and refresh the page. The new recordings and sound visualizations should appear.
upload.admin: Add tech@gainforest.net as a project administrator if you would like GainForest to help organize the data.

finish.heading: Ready for the field?
finish.text: Run the equipment checklist once more, pack spare batteries and keep the recorder ID with your field notes.
